mnemonics: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away (Layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application)
keyPoints: The OSI model has 7 layers, while the TCP/IP model has 4 layers.
The Transport Layer is responsible for end-to-end communication and error recovery.
Protocols like HTTP, FTP, and SMTP operate at the Application Layer in both models.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Network Layer (routing) with the Transport Layer (end-to-end delivery).
Assuming the TCP/IP Application Layer maps directly to just the OSI Application Layer, when it actually encompasses Session and Presentation layers too.
